review.citation: van Rijn RM, et al. Computed tomography for the diagnosis of lumbar spinal pathology in adult patients with low back pain or sciatica: a diagnostic systematic review. European Spine Journal. 2012.

identity.title: Computed tomography for the diagnosis of lumbar spinal pathology in adult patients with low back pain or sciatica: a diagnostic systematic review

question.naturalLanguageInput: How accurately does computed tomography diagnose lumbar disc herniation in adults with low back pain or sciatica?
question.structuredInput: In adults with low back pain, radicular leg pain, or sciatica who are suspected of lumbar disc herniation, evaluate computed tomography as the index test against surgery or an expert reference standard for diagnostic sensitivity and specificity; prioritize diagnostic accuracy studies.
eligibilityCriteria: Adults with low back pain, radicular pain, or sciatica
Computed tomography used to diagnose lumbar disc herniation
Surgery or a clinically acceptable expert reference standard
Reports data usable to assess diagnostic accuracy

population: adults with low back pain or sciatica

identity.title: CT of lumbar spine disk herniation: correlation with surgical findings

The neuroradiographic diagnosis of lumbar herniated nucleus pulposus: II. A comparison of computed tomography (CT), myelography, CT-myelography, and magnetic resonance imaging
The value of myelography, computerized tomography and course of pain for the diagnosis of recurrent herniated disk
Lumbar disk herniation: diagnosis with CT or myelography
Disk-caused nerve compression in patients with acute low-back pain: diagnosis with MR, CT myelography, and plain CT
